rulings.Case Title: SEHAT SUTARDJA VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF SEHAT SUTARDJA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND EVIDENCE. Hearing required. Counsel are requested to be prepared to address whether the motion should be denied because Plaintiff has not met his initial burden on summary judgment of showing that the FTB has adopted the "FTB Stock Option Regulation" defined in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC"). The FAC pleads at paragraphs 8 and 12 the "general policies" that comprise the FTB Stock Option Regulation. Plaintiff does not appear to have presented any evidence to support that the FTB has adopted as a regulation the part of the FTB Stock Option Regulation described in paragraph 12. The only "undisputed material fact" ("UMF") submitted by Plaintiff to establish that Defendant adopted the FTB Stock Option Regulation as defined in the FAC is that Defendant "adopted the ¿ Office of the Chief Counsel Internal Revenue Service Memorandum Number 200728042 as an enforcement for policy for Section 409A in the State of California." (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Fact no. 8.) The IRS Memo 200728042 cited in Plaintiff's UMF no. 8 does not address the part of the FTB Stock Option Regulation plead in paragraph 12. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW ARENA VS. MARK PIERRE ROBINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 7, 2014, LINE 1, DEFENDANT MARK ROBINSON Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED AND GOOD CAUSE HAS BEEN SHOWN; ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER DISMISSING THIS MATTER AND OPPOSING SANCTIONS. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 88 TOWNSEND STREET OWNERS ASOCIATION, VS. 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT LLC, et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT, LLC and CANNON CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiff's eighth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is sustained with ten days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff an opportunity to allege with the requisite specificity facts that show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were made. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Additionally, Plaintiff must allege specific facts establishing that Defendants' had no reasonable ground for believing the representation to be true. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: QUALITY INTERNATIONAL PACKAGING, A CORPORATION VS. WORLD OF CHARMS INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint Of Plaintiff Quality International
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, Line 7: PLAINTIFF QUALITY INTERNATIONAL PACKAGING'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint Of Plaintiff Quality International. The motion is granted in part. Plaintiff has leave to add causes of action 3 through 7 against defendant World of Charms, Inc. and Sasimu LLC, Simayof, Inc., and Simus Inc. The court denies the motion as to Sarit Simayof-Cohen, Adi Simayof, and Yedidia Simayof. Defendant's evidence indicates that plaintiff knew or should have known of their potential liability earlier and allowing amendment a mere 4 days before the trial date would be prejudicial. In addition, if plaintiff recovers against defendant(s), plaintiff can bring a post judgment motion pursuant to CCP section 187 to add the individuals as additional alter ego judgment debtors. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AFI INVESTMENT, INC VS. C3 PATIENTS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 7, 2014, Line 12: DEFENDANT C3 PATIENTS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. The motion to quash service of the summons and complaint is denied. First, defendant appears to be an unincorporated association and, as such, must be represented by counsel in order to appear in court. The entity cannot represent itself in pro per. See Clean Air Transport System v. San Mateo (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 576, 578. In any event, plaintiff establishes proper service of the summons and complaint on defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
